top_candidates.reasoning: Three verse-specific markers: (1) the explicit pivot reference — the engineering body says 'this is the verse that closes the karma-yoga argument and pivots the chapter' and references Arjuna's 2.54 question verbatim; (2) the layered niścalā + acalā distinction is rendered as 'her buddhi is niścalā — not pulled by external claims — and acalā — not internally re-litigating once the pulls have been resisted'; (3) the grammatically-forward closing condition (tadā yogam avāpsyasi → 'THEN you will attain yoga') is named explicitly. No other verse in the chapter has all three markers.

Adjacent verse on buddhi-yoga maturation. 2.52 names the thicket-crossing; 2.53 names the closing condition. The analog's pivot reference and the niścalā+acalā layering would not fit 2.52, which concerns the transition rather than the resulting state.
BG 6.20 ('when the mind, restrained by yoga, attains stillness') extends the niścalā framing. Distant possibility — the chapter-context and 2.54-pivot references in the analog tie it firmly to 2.53.
